feature: Inventory and dependency graph: organization model / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on organization model cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for organization model: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover organization model, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for organization model.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for organization model.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance organization model inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: organization-model_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for organization model cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for organization model; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for organization model.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for organization model, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for organization model.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for organization model.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing organization model data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling organization model inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for organization model.
acceptance_criteria: Given organization model data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: organization model connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for team model: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for team model scalability posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for team model.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for team model.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance team model automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: team-model_scalability_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for team model scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for team model; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for team model.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for team model recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for team model.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for team model.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing team model data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling team model automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for team model.
acceptance_criteria: Given team model data exists, when the scalability automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: team model connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for approval workflow: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect approval workflow operational-excellence behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for approval workflow.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for approval workflow critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance approval workflow tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: approval-workflow_operational-excellence_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for approval workflow operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for approval workflow; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for approval workflow.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for approval workflow recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for approval workflow.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for approval workflow.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing approval workflow data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling approval workflow tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for approval workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Given approval workflow data exists, when the operational-excellence tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: approval workflow connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for API tokens: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover API tokens, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the disaster-recovery pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for API tokens.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for API tokens.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance API tokens inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: api-tokens_disaster-recovery_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for API tokens disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for API tokens; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for API tokens.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for API tokens recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for API tokens.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for API tokens.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing API tokens data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling API tokens inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for API tokens.
acceptance_criteria: Given API tokens data exists, when the disaster-recovery inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: API tokens connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for service accounts: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for service accounts with scalability guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for service accounts.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for service accounts.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance service accounts operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: service-accounts_scalability_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for service accounts scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for service accounts; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for service accounts.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for service accounts recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for service accounts.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for service accounts.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing service accounts data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling service accounts operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for service accounts.
acceptance_criteria: Given service accounts data exists, when the scalability operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: service accounts connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for data residency: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect data residency operational-excellence behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for data residency.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for data residency critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance data residency tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: data-residency_operational-excellence_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for data residency operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for data residency; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for data residency.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for data residency recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for data residency.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for data residency.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing data residency data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling data residency tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for data residency.
acceptance_criteria: Given data residency data exists, when the operational-excellence tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: data residency connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for organization settings: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize organization settings evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for scalability issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for organization settings.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for organization settings.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Tenant, RBAC, and Governance organization settings ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: organization-settings_scalability_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for organization settings scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for organization settings; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for organization settings.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for organization settings recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for organization settings.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for organization settings.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing organization settings data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling organization settings ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for organization settings.
acceptance_criteria: Given organization settings data exists, when the scalability ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: organization settings connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
